explanations.12: matchPath is checking if the current URL matches the pattern '/blog/tags/:slug', where ':slug' is a variable part of the URL we want to capture.
explanations.16: The question mark after 'match' means 'only try to access params.slug if match exists', so we don't crash if the URL didn't match the pattern.
explanations.36: This helper function handles tags that can be either plain strings or objects with a label property — it extracts whichever format is being used.
explanations.42: We're converting the tag's display name to a URL-friendly slug and comparing it against the slug from the URL to find matching tags.
explanations.55: Subtracting dates in descending order sorts posts from newest to oldest — the bigger date value comes first.
